title: Modern Node CJS require(esm) may return a namespace object, not ERR_REQUIRE_ESM
summary: Agents often claim that requiring an ESM-only package from CommonJS always throws ERR_REQUIRE_ESM. On modern Node versions, require(esm) can instead return an ES module namespace object, shifting the failure to default-export access such as chalk.blue is not a function.
ai_value: Before changing module systems or pinning old packages, an agent should inspect Node version, resolved package version, and the actual require() return shape.

trigger_signals.description: CommonJS code calls require('chalk') and then chalk.blue(...) while package metadata resolves chalk@5+.
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: Do not assume the only possible failure is ERR_REQUIRE_ESM; inspect the returned module shape and Node version.

trigger_signals.description: Runtime error says TypeError: chalk.blue is not a function.
trigger_signals.pattern: TypeError: chalk\.blue is not a function
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: This may be a namespace/default export mismatch rather than a plain missing dependency.

negative_signals.description: The project already imports chalk using ESM import syntax.
negative_signals.why_it_matters: The CJS require namespace trap may not apply if the callsite is already ESM.

negative_signals.description: The installed chalk version is v4 or lower.
negative_signals.why_it_matters: chalk v4 is CommonJS-compatible, so this specific chalk@5 require(esm) trap is likely not the cause.

common_wrong_assumptions: All ESM-only packages always fail from CJS with ERR_REQUIRE_ESM.
The correct fix is always to pin chalk to v4.
If require('chalk') does not throw, then chalk.blue must be available as a top-level property.

first_checks.why: Node's CJS/ESM interop behavior changes by version.

first_checks.check: Check the installed package version and resolved package metadata.
first_checks.why: The same package name can have CJS and ESM-major versions.

first_checks.check: Inspect what require() actually returns before rewriting broad module settings.
first_checks.why: Modern Node may return a namespace object with a default export.

decision_rules.condition: require('chalk') returns an object with a callable default export and code uses chalk.blue(...).
decision_rules.then: Use the default export shape, migrate this callsite to import syntax, or convert the project/module boundary intentionally; do not diagnose this as a plain ERR_REQUIRE_ESM case.
Use dynamic import(), migrate the relevant module to ESM, or pin a CJS-compatible version only as an explicit compatibility tradeoff.

do_not: Do not state that CJS require() of ESM always throws ERR_REQUIRE_ESM without checking Node version.
Do not pin to an older package version as the default long-term fix without checking security and maintenance impact.
Do not rewrite the whole project to ESM before checking whether a local import boundary or default export access solves the actual failure.

agent_directives.preferred_next_step: Diagnose the active Node version, installed package version, and require() return shape before changing dependencies or module settings.

risk.notes: Changing module system broadly or pinning packages can create project-wide churn; keep the first action diagnostic.

sources.excerpt: Node.js official documentation section on interoperability with CommonJS.
sources.supports: Node documents interoperability boundaries between ECMAScript modules and CommonJS.

sources.excerpt: Node.js official documentation section on loading ECMAScript modules using require().
sources.supports: Node documents loading ECMAScript modules using require() under supported conditions.

sources.excerpt: Local command observed TypeError: chalk.blue is not a function under Node v22.22.2 and chalk@5.6.2.
sources.supports: Local verification observed chalk@5.6.2 under Node v22.22.2 returning a namespace object and failing at chalk.blue access.

verification_runs.command: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && cd "$tmp" && npm init -y && npm i chalk@5 && printf "const chalk = require('chalk'); console.log(chalk.blue('x'));\n" > test.js && node test.js
verification_runs.observed_result: TypeError: chalk.blue is not a function, not ERR_REQUIRE_ESM.
verification_runs.conclusion: The stale agent answer 'ESM-only in CJS always throws ERR_REQUIRE_ESM' is not reliable for Node v22 + chalk@5.6.2.

verification_runs.command: tmp=$(mktemp -d) && cd "$tmp" && npm init -y && npm i chalk@5 && printf "const chalk = require('chalk'); console.log('node', process.version); console.log('shape', Object.keys(chalk).slice(0,10)); console.log('default-type', typeof chalk.default); console.log(chalk.blue('x'));\n" > test.js && node test.js
verification_runs.observed_result: Node v22.22.2 and chalk@5.6.2: require('chalk') returned a namespace object containing default; typeof default was function; chalk.blue threw TypeError; require('chalk').default.blue('ok') succeeded in a follow-up check.
verification_runs.conclusion: The trap remains current in a fresh local run and the default export shape is the minimal diagnosis before broad dependency or module-system changes.
